title: erodium pilosum var. chaerophyllum
intro: Erodium pilosum var. chaerophyllum, also known as the corn-marigold or heron's bill, is a variety of the Erodium pilosum plant, belonging to the geranium family and characterized by its white or pink flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Erodium pilosum var. chaerophyllum include soothing digestive problems, reducing inflammation, and alleviating menstrual cramps, among others. This plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it can also be used as a topical application for skin irritations and wounds.
